skill.name: app-store-optimization
skill.description: Complete App Store Optimization (ASO) toolkit for researching, optimizing, and tracking mobile app performance on Apple App Store and Google Play Store
skill.summary: Complete App Store Optimization (ASO) toolkit for researching, optimizing, and tracking mobile app performance on Apple App Store and Google Play Store
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 432 static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown code fences as shell commands, hash-related ASO terminology as weak cryptography, and variable names containing 'request' as network activity. This is a legitimate App Store Optimization skill with no security concerns.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Scanner detected hash-related keywords in ASO code (hash functions, hash tables for keyword scoring). No cryptographic code present. This is standard ASO terminology for keyword hashing and scoring algorithms.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 259 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' are false positives caused by the scanner detecting hash-related ASO terminology (keyword hash tables, hash functions for scoring). No actual cryptographic algorithms are used.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected backticks in markdown files. These are markdown code fence delimiters (```), not Ruby shell execution. Documentation files contain example JSON/JSON code blocks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 163 instances of 'external_commands' are false positives. The backticks are markdown syntax for code blocks in HOW_TO_USE.md, README.md, and SKILL.md - no actual shell commands present.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Hidden File Access (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected file paths with dots in markdown documentation. No actual hidden file access occurring - these are references to example file paths in documentation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The 5 filesystem findings are false positives. References to '.md' files in documentation are not actual hidden file access attempts.

low_findings.title: Python HTTP Libraries (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner detected variable names containing 'request' but no actual network calls. The skill processes local data and generates recommendations - no external API calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The 5 'network' findings are false positives. Variable names like 'feature_requests' and 'request_text' are local data processing, not HTTP requests. No urllib, requests, or HTTP libraries are used.

content.value_statement: Mobile app developers struggle to get their apps discovered in crowded app stores. This ASO toolkit provides keyword research, competitor analysis, metadata optimization, and performance tracking to maximize app visibility and downloads on Apple App Store and Google Play.
actual_capabilities: Research keyword volume, competition, and relevance for app discovery
Analyze competitor ASO strategies and identify market gaps
Optimize app metadata with platform-specific character limits
Calculate ASO health scores across multiple performance dimensions
Analyze user reviews for sentiment, issues, and feature requests
Generate localization strategies for global app reach
limitations: Does not provide real-time app store data or rankings
No direct integration with Apple App Store Connect or Google Play Console
Keyword search volume estimates are directional, not actual store data
Does not submit or manage app listings on behalf of users

use_cases.title: Pre-Launch App Optimization
use_cases.description: New app developers use the skill to research keywords, analyze competitors, and create optimized metadata before submitting to app stores.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile app developers preparing new app launches

use_cases.title: Existing App ASO Audit
use_cases.description: Established app owners use the skill to audit their current ASO performance, identify weaknesses, and receive optimization recommendations.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and app marketers improving existing apps

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use the skill to monitor competitor strategies, track keyword gaps, and inform product positioning decisions.
use_cases.target_user: Growth marketers and competitive intelligence analysts

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Hey Claude—I just added the "app-store-optimization" skill. Can you research the best keywords for my productivity app? I'm targeting professionals who need task management and team collaboration features.
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial keyword research for a new app

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Hey Claude—I just added the "app-store-optimization" skill. Can you analyze keywords that Todoist, Asana, and Monday.com are using? I want to find gaps and opportunities for my project management app.

prompt_templates.title: Metadata Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Hey Claude—I just added the "app-store-optimization" skill. Can you optimize my app title for the Apple App Store? My app is called "TaskFlow" and I want to rank for "task manager", "productivity", and "team collaboration". The title needs to be under 30 characters.
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimizing app title with character limits

prompt_templates.title: Full ASO Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Hey Claude—I just added the "app-store-optimization" skill. Can you perform a complete ASO audit for my fitness app? I want to understand my keyword rankings, metadata quality, competitor landscape, and get a comprehensive health score with recommendations.

output_examples.input: Research keywords for a productivity app targeting professionals
output_examples.output: Keyword recommendations include primary terms (task manager, productivity app) with search volume estimates, competition analysis (low/medium/high), and strategic recommendations for primary vs secondary keyword targeting.

output_examples.input: Optimize app metadata for Apple App Store
output_examples.output: Title suggestions: 'TaskFlow - AI Manager' (28 chars), Subtitle: 'Team Productivity' (18 chars), Keyword field: 'task,productivity,team,organize,planner', with character count validation and keyword density analysis.

Overall score: 72/100 (Good). Breakdown: Metadata Quality 18/25, Ratings & Reviews 20/25, Keyword Performance 17/25, Conversion Metrics 17/25. Top recommendations: Add more long-tail keywords, improve description length.

best_practices: Research keywords before naming your app - include primary keywords in the title while maintaining brand identity
Use platform-specific character limits as guidance, not strict rules - clarity matters more than keyword stuffing
Update metadata regularly based on performance data and seasonal trends, not just at launch
anti_patterns: Do not stuff keywords into titles or descriptions in a way that harms readability
Do not ignore platform differences - Apple and Google have different optimization requirements
Do not set and forget - ASO requires ongoing monitoring and iteration based on performance data

faq.question: How does this skill get keyword search volume data?
faq.answer: The skill provides estimated keyword metrics based on general market knowledge. For actual search volumes, connect to App Store Connect or Google Play Console analytics.

faq.question: Can this skill submit my app to the stores?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates optimized metadata recommendations. You must manually submit or use platform-specific APIs to update your app listing.

faq.question: Does it work for both Apple App Store and Google Play?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports both platforms with platform-specific recommendations for character limits, keyword fields, and optimization strategies.

faq.question: How often should I update my app metadata?
faq.answer: Review and update metadata quarterly or when you release significant features. Major updates warrant new 'What's New' sections.

faq.question: Can I use this for games as well as apps?
faq.answer: Yes, the metadata optimization principles apply to games. The skill includes guidance for game-specific elements like screenshots and preview videos.

faq.question: What is a good ASO health score?
faq.answer: A score above 70/100 is considered good. Scores above 85 indicate excellent optimization. Focus on improving your lowest-scoring category first.
